response:
THE DESIGNER IS CREATING A PROMOTIONAL POSTER WITH A CENTRAL CIRCULAR ELEMENT. THE TOTAL AREA OF THE 8 CONCENTRIC CIRCLES CAN BE CALCULATED BY SUMMING THE AREAS OF EACH INDIVIDUAL CIRCLE. EACH SUBSEQUENT CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS INCREASED BY 10% COMPARED TO THE PREVIOUS ONE. THE FORMULA FOR THE AREA OF A CIRCLE IS πR², WHERE R IS THE RADIUS.  

THE RADIUS OF THE FIRST CIRCLE IS R. THE RADIUS OF THE SECOND CIRCLE IS 1.1R, THE THIRD IS (1.1)²R, AND SO ON, UNTIL THE EIGHTH CIRCLE, WHICH HAS A RADIUS OF (1.1)⁷R. THE TOTAL AREA IS THE SUM OF πR², π(1.1R)², π(1.1)⁴R², ..., π(1.1)¹⁴R². THIS IS A GEOMETRIC SERIES WITH A COMMON RATIO OF (1.1)² = 1.21. THE SUM OF THE SERIES IS πR² × [1 - (1.21)⁸] / [1 - 1.21]. THIS GIVES THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES AS πR² × [1 - (1.21)⁸] / (-0.21).  

THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS 2πR₅, WHERE R₅ IS THE RADIUS OF THE 5TH CIRCLE. SINCE EACH CIRCLE INCREASES BY 10%, R₅ = (1.1)⁴R = 1.4641R. THE CIRCUMFERENCE IS THEN 2π × 1.4641R = 2.9282πR. THIS LENGTH IS TO BE DIVIDED INTO SEGMENTS OF 0.5 CM EACH TO PLACE CHARACTERS. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS THE FLOOR OF (2.9282πR) / 0.5, WHICH MUST BE AN INTEGER. TO ENSURE THIS, THE VALUE OF R MUST BE SELECTED SUCH THAT THE RESULT IS A WHOLE NUMBER. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE PLACED
